app.name: IslamFinance
home.prayerPrompt: Open Prayer Times to set your location
home.prayerTomorrow: All prayers done for today — see you at Fajr
learn.emptySubtitle: Articles will appear here as calculators are added.
history.emptySubtitle: Your past calculations will be saved here.
onboarding.language.title: Choose your language
onboarding.language.subtitle: You can change this later in Settings.
onboarding.madhab.title: Choose your madhab
onboarding.madhab.subtitle: Calculations follow the school you choose. You can change it anytime in Settings.
onboarding.currency.title: Choose your currency
onboarding.currency.subtitle: Your default currency for amounts. Changeable in Settings.
zakat.inputSubtitle: Enter your assets in {{currency}}. Leave anything that doesn't apply at 0.
zakat.priceHint: Enter today's gold and silver price per gram in your currency — used for the nisab threshold and to value any metals you hold.
zakat.settingsHint: Using the {{madhab}} school and {{basis}} nisab basis. Change these in Settings.
zakat.err.goldPrice: Enter the current gold price per gram
zakat.err.silverPrice: Enter the current silver price per gram
zakat.field.personalDebtsDue: Debts due within the lunar year
zakat.field.haulComplete: Has a lunar year passed since your wealth reached nisab?
zakat.result.title: Your Zakat
zakat.result.haulIncomplete: Haul not complete — no Zakat due yet
zakat.result.citation: Calculated at {{rate}}% on wealth above the {{basis}} nisab ({{grams}}g), with jewelry handled per the {{madhab}} school. Source: docs/fiqh/zakat.md.
zakat.result.disclaimer: These results are not yet scholar-verified and are provisional. Please confirm with a qualified scholar before acting on them.
zakat.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Zakat calculator to see your result here.
inheritance.inputSubtitle: Enter the net estate in {{currency}} and the number of each surviving heir.
inheritance.estateHint: Enter the net estate — after funeral costs, debts, and any bequests (up to one-third). Leave heirs that don't apply at 0 / No.
inheritance.settingsHint: Using the {{madhab}} school. Change it in Settings.
inheritance.unsupported: This combination of heirs isn't supported yet. Please consult a qualified scholar for this case.
inheritance.err.spouseConflict: A person cannot have both a husband and wives
inheritance.result.title: Inheritance shares
inheritance.result.citation: Distributed by fixed (farā'iḍ) shares per the {{madhab}} school. Source: docs/fiqh/inheritance.md.
inheritance.result.awlNote: Shares exceeded the estate, so all were reduced proportionally (ʿAwl).
inheritance.result.raddNote: A surplus remained with no residuary heir, so it was returned to the fixed-share heirs (Radd).
inheritance.result.baytAlMalNote: A surplus remained that this school does not return to the heirs — classically it goes to the public treasury (Bayt al-Māl), so the shares below total less than the estate.
inheritance.result.disclaimer: These results are not yet scholar-verified and are provisional. Inheritance is legally and religiously consequential — confirm with a qualified scholar (and, for a valid will, a lawyer) before acting.
inheritance.result.emptySubtitle: Run the inheritance calculator to see the shares here.
fitrana.inputSubtitle: Zakat al-Fitr, in {{currency}}, paid before the Eid prayer.
fitrana.hint: One ṣāʿ (about 2.5 kg) of a staple food per person, or its cash value. Many mosques announce a set amount per person — enter that, or estimate it from a food's price.
fitrana.result.title: Your Fitrana
fitrana.result.citation: One ṣāʿ of staple food per person (½ ṣāʿ of wheat for Hanafi), paid before the Eid prayer. Source: docs/fiqh/fitrana.md.
fitrana.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. Confirm the per-person amount with your local mosque or a scholar.
fitrana.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Fitrana calculator to see your result here.
qurbani.hint: Based on the {{madhab}} school. A sheep/goat = 1 share; a cow or camel = 7 shares. The ruling and whether it's per-person or per-household depend on your school.
qurbani.result.citation: Sheep/goat = 1 share, cow/camel = 7. Ruling per your school. Source: docs/fiqh/qurbani.md.
qurbani.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. Confirm eligibility and the animal's conditions with a scholar.
qurbani.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Qurbani check to see your result here.
fidya.hint: Fidya = feed one poor person for each fast you cannot make up. Kaffarah = feed 60 poor people for each fast deliberately broken. Enter the cost to feed one person for a day (your local figure).
fidya.field.perDayAmount: Cost to feed one person (per day)
fidya.err.priceRequired: Enter the amount to feed one person
fidya.result.title: Expiation total
fidya.result.citationFidya: Fidya = feeding one poor person per missed fast (Qur'an 2:184). Source: docs/fiqh/fidya.md.
fidya.result.citationKaffarah: Kaffarah = feeding 60 poor people per deliberately-broken fast (Bukhari & Muslim). Source: docs/fiqh/fidya.md.
fidya.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. Kaffarah's order (fasting vs feeding) and special cases (pregnancy, illness) should be confirmed with a scholar.
fidya.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Fidya/Kaffarah calculator to see your result here.
livestock.inputSubtitle: Zakat on grazing livestock — camels, cattle, and sheep/goats. Paid in-kind.
livestock.hint: Using the {{madhab}} school. Zakat here is paid as animals, by the classical niṣāb tables. Most schools count only free-grazing herds owned for a full lunar year.
livestock.err.maxCount: That's more animals than this supports
livestock.field.hawlMet: Owned for a full lunar year?
livestock.reason.hawl-not-met: A lunar year hasn't passed — none due yet
livestock.reason.working-exempt: Working animals — exempt in your school
livestock.result.title: Livestock Zakat
livestock.result.valueAllowed: The animal, or its cash value
livestock.result.citation: In-kind zakat by the classical niṣāb tables (Abū Bakr's zakat letter, Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī 1454). Source: docs/fiqh/livestock.md.
livestock.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. Confirm the animals due (and any cash-value option) with a scholar.
livestock.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Livestock Zakat calculator to see your result here.
ushr.inputSubtitle: Zakat on harvested crops. No lunar year — it falls due at harvest.
ushr.hint: Using the {{madhab}} school. 10% if watered by rain/springs (no cost), 5% if irrigated by wells/pumps/machinery, 7.5% if both. Most schools apply it only above ~653 kg; the Hanafi school applies it to any amount.
ushr.result.title: Your Ushr
ushr.result.belowNisab: Below niṣāb (~{{nisab}} kg) — no Ushr due
ushr.result.citation: 10% rain-fed / 5% irrigated / 7.5% both, on the harvest (Qur'an 6:141; Bukhari 1483). Niṣāb 5 awsuq ≈ {{nisab}} kg (majority; Hanafi none). Source: docs/fiqh/ushr.md.
ushr.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. Confirm the niṣāb, whether to deduct costs, and which crops apply with a scholar.
ushr.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Ushr calculator to see your result here.
qada.inputSubtitle: Count the prayers or fasts you owe (qaḍāʾ).
qada.hint: Using the {{madhab}} school. Prayers: 5 a day (6 in Hanafi, incl. Witr); a woman's menstruation days aren't counted. Fasts: made up one-for-one; if delayed past the next Ramadan, most schools add fidya. Enter the period (prayers) or the number of fasts.
qada.err.minPeriod: Enter a period to make up
qada.result.title: Qaḍāʾ
qada.result.fidyaAlso: Fidya also due — see the Fidya calculator
qada.result.citationPrayers: Five daily fard prayers (six in Hanafi, incl. Witr); menstruation days excluded for women (Muslim 335). Source: docs/fiqh/qada.md.
qada.result.citationFasts: Ramadan fasts made up one-for-one; fidya added for makeup delayed past the next Ramadan (majority). Source: docs/fiqh/qada.md.
qada.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. These counts are estimates (especially the menstruation average); confirm your situation with a scholar.
qada.result.emptySubtitle: Run the Qaḍāʾ calculator to see your result here.
purification.inputSubtitle: Cleanse the impermissible portion of your investment income, in {{currency}}.
purification.hint: Halal-screened stocks can still carry a small share of impermissible income (e.g. interest). Purify it by giving that portion to charity — separately from Zakat, and without expecting reward. Get the non-compliant % (or per-share impure income) from a stock-screening report.
purification.result.title: Purification
purification.result.citation: Purify the non-permissible share of investment income by giving it to charity, per AAOIFI Shari'ah Standard 21. It is not Zakat and carries no reward. Source: docs/fiqh/purification.md.
purification.result.disclaimer: Not yet scholar-verified — provisional. Screening ratios and whether capital gains need purifying vary by scholar; confirm your figures and method.
purification.result.emptySubtitle: Run the purification calculator to see your result here.
hijri.subtitle: Islamic date converter & events
hijri.note: Dates are calculated and may differ from local moon-sighting by a day. Use +/− to adjust.
prayer.subtitle: Prayer times & Qibla for your location
prayer.needLocation: Allow location to get accurate prayer times and Qibla direction.
prayer.denied: Location permission was denied. Enable it in your device settings, then try again.
prayer.compassNote: Hold your phone flat, away from metal. Compass accuracy varies by device.
tasbih.subtitle: Digital dhikr counter
reminders.subtitle: Prayer & Islamic date notifications
reminders.prayerDesc: A daily notification at each prayer time.
reminders.eventsDesc: A heads-up for Ramadan, the Eids, and other key dates.
reminders.needLocation: Set your location in Prayer Times first.
reminders.note: Reminders use on-device scheduled notifications (no internet). Prayer times are based on your last location and may drift over the year — reopen this screen to refresh.
reminders.denied: Notifications are turned off. Enable them in your device settings, then try again.
